name: Nike Extended
description: Nike, the winged goddess of victory, is more than just a symbol of triumph. In this extended interpretation, Nike embodies not only the achievement itself but also the process, the perseverance, and the enduring legacy of success. Born from the Titan Pallas and the goddess Styx, she is a sibling to Zelus (zeal), Kratos (strength), and Bia (force), representing the essential components needed to attain victory. Nike's role extends beyond the battlefield; she presides over athletic competitions, artistic endeavors, and any pursuit where excellence is the goal. She is often depicted with wings, symbolizing her ability to swiftly deliver victory, and carries a wreath or palm branch as a token of achievement. Her association with Zeus is strong, often serving as his charioteer or standing beside him, emphasizing the divine endorsement of just victories. While victories can be fleeting, Nike's extended influence encompasses the enduring impact of those triumphs, inspiring future generations and shaping the course of history. She represents the idea that true victory lies not just in the moment of triumph, but in the lasting positive consequences it creates. Furthermore, she embodies the ethical considerations of victory, advocating for triumphs achieved through honorable means and for the responsible use of power gained through success. Her influence extends to modern concepts of sportsmanship, fair play, and the pursuit of excellence in all fields.
subtitle: Winged Goddess of Enduring Triumph

keyMyths.title: Nike's Birth and Allegiance
keyMyths.description: Nike was born from the Titan Pallas and Styx, and immediately allied herself with Zeus during the Titanomachy. This choice ensured her association with divine power and just victories.

keyMyths.title: Nike as Zeus's Charioteer
keyMyths.description: Nike frequently serves as the charioteer of Zeus, driving his chariot and symbolizing his dominion over the heavens and his victories. This highlights her closeness to the king of the gods.

keyMyths.title: Nike's Presence in Athletic Games
keyMyths.description: Nike presides over athletic competitions, awarding victors with wreaths and symbolizing the pursuit of excellence and triumph in physical endeavors. Her presence inspires athletes to strive for their best.

sources.author: Hesiod
sources.description: Account of the origins of the Greek gods and goddesses, including Nike's parentage.

sources.author: Pausanias
